title: Aadat

description: A defining anthem of the 2000s South Asian indie-rock scene, 'Aadat' is celebrated for its iconic melancholic arpeggiated guitar intro and soulful, high-pitched vocals. It captured the raw angst of heartbreak and became a massive cross-border hit, bridging the music spaces of Pakistan and India.
trivia: The song was originally recorded by Atif Aslam and Goher Mumtaz in a local Pakistani studio for a mere 3,000 Pakistani rupees.
An unreleased version of the song leaked on the internet in 2003, becoming a viral sensation across college campuses and file-sharing networks before the official album was even released.
The overwhelming success of the song paradoxically led to a split within the band, prompting Atif Aslam to launch his solo career and Farhan Saeed to step in as Jal's new lead vocalist.
Its immense popularity in India led filmmaker Mahesh Bhatt to license the track for the Bollywood movie 'Kalyug' (2005), which officially introduced Atif Aslam's voice to the Indian film industry.
